purpose: Phase 6 compares five candidate pool formulas under continuous engine to identify which solves Agi-dominance. Trigger: Phase 5 confirmed continuous engine alone doesn't resolve dominance (97.5% conditional Fast vs Strong); Jordan asked to compare dominance-solver options.

entries.section: §dice — derived from face distribution at TN 7
§Damage Formula — Light Blade Cut vs None
L91 (PP-717 D3) — net >=4 discrete, magnitude >=4 continuous
entries.quoted_text: Critical Hit (net hits >= 4): weapon modifier doubled before applying armour reduction. (PP-717 — raised from >= 3.)

entries.section: PP-612 (the formula PP-612 attempted; PP-615 reversed it)
entries.quoted_text: PP-612 concluded Combat Pool uses non-doubled formula (Agi + hist + 3); PP-615 explicitly rejected this. Formula included here as a proposal candidate for sim comparison; not current canon.

entries.section: L14 §Pool Formula — Phase 6 proposal variants derived from canonical formula; not canon themselves
entries.quoted_text: Cap value 14 chosen because probability of >=1 success saturates near 12D (per params/core.md L88-94); 14D allows 1 wound penalty before reaching saturation.

entries.section: L14 §Pool Formula — Phase 6 proposal variants derived from canonical formula; not canon themselves
entries.quoted_text: Cap value 12 chosen as tighter alternative to cap-14, exploring whether more aggressive ceiling addresses dominance better.

entries.section: PP-717 D2 — ratified in v22-v27 weapon sim, reverted by Decision A 2026-05-15
entries.quoted_text: Combat Pool = min(Agi,4)*2 + max(0,Agi-4)*1 + Hist + 3 — diminishing returns above Agi 4

entries.section: Architecture C — sim baseline matching v27 + Phase 4 + Phase 5
entries.quoted_text: Architecture C — sim consistency with v27/Phase 4/Phase 5. Canonical formula is End*5 per params/combat.md L15.

sim_limitations: Strike-only (no Feint, ED, Distance, Initiative) — disables action triangle counter-play, may inflate dominance numbers
Single weapon (Light Blade Cut) and single armour (None)
Continuous engine assumes Normal approximation — validated by Phase 5 distribution equivalence test
key_finding: Pool formula alone does NOT solve dominance. All five candidates produce >=72% conditional Fast win — above the 65% dominance threshold. End-dominance is essentially formula-independent (99.6-99.9% across all formulas). The structural drivers are wound-spiral feedback, HP/stamina-window asymmetry, and critical-hit cascade — not pool sizing. Next sim should test action triangle (Feint/Defend) counter-play or wound-spiral mitigation.
